scaled_oracle_cases.description: Verify parsing of a THetA file with two tumor fractions, multi-sample C and p* fields.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: The function must correctly parse mu into one normal fraction and two tumor fractions, C into a 2x2 list of copy numbers, and p* into three probabilities.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old seed behavior would incorrectly parse C and p* as flat lists or misalign values.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
sample = "NLL\tmu\tC\tp*\n15.2\t0.2,0.5,0.3\t2:3,4:1\t0.1,0.2,0.7\n"
fname = "theta_multi.txt"
with open(fname, "w") as f:
    f.write(sample)
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a test with the multi-tumor file and compare the output dictionary.
Extend input data to contain multiple tumor fractions (two tumors) in the mu, C, and p* fields, testing parsing of multi-sample THetA results.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Verify parsing of a THetA file with placeholder '?' in C and 'A' in p*.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: The function must convert non-numeric alphabetic placeholders to None in C and p* fields.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: For input '10.0\t0.5,0.5\t2:?\t0.3,A', the output dictionary must have C=[[2, None]] and p*=[0.3, None].
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: Old seed behavior would crash on int('?') or float('A') or produce incorrect values.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
sample = "NLL\tmu\tC\tp*\n10.0\t0.5,0.5\t2:?\t0.3,A\n"
fname = "theta_placeholder.txt"
with open(fname, "w") as f:
    f.write(sample)
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a test with placeholder values and compare the output dictionary.
Introduce placeholder values (e.g., '?' or alphabetic strings) in the C and p* fields to test handling of missing or non-numeric data.
